title: The High Renaissance in Florence and Rome
description: Test your knowledge of the High Renaissance in Florence and Rome.
topic_path: The High Renaissance in Florence and Rome

assessment_items.question_content:
In the colossal statue of *David*, Michelangelo created a(n) $\_\_\_\_\_\_$ body.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d20bee76c168e6abc628f58ef470f660.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Michelangelo represents not a real body but an idealized, perfect one. Ideal beauty is one of the characteristics of High Renaissance art.

assessment_items.question_content:
**When Martin Luther nailed his 95 Theses to the door of a church in 1517, he initiated the . . .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Protestant Reformation threatened the dominance and future of the Catholic Church, leaving Europe in turmoil.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In Raphael's *Alba Madonna*, the traditional iconography of the divine was replaced with symbols derived from classical mythology.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6226cb4d31e9c7fbc254e9907410941b.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Symbols of the divine such as the heavy golden halos popular in 15th Century paintings have been replaced with the beauty of the natural world.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which features of the Libyan Sibyl are typical of Michelangelo's figures?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/039b92a4d05726b5a08b0ad71c2edaa8.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The twisted pose, anatomical detail, idealized beauty, and use of a classical figure to reinforce Christian ideas are all typical of Michelanegelo's work.

choices.content: the use of a classical figure to reinforce Christian ideas

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the *Virgin of the Rocks*, Leonardo's interest in the details of the natural world is obscured by his use of sfumato.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d1b1faed1da64de8605be7ac876e50e0.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Leonardo pays particular attention to anatomical, botanical, and geological details which remain clear despite his use of sfumato (a smokey haziness that softens outlines).

assessment_items.question_content:
**Ancient Greeks thought that geometry was  . . .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Ancient Greeks thought that geometry was a vehicle through which we could imagine the perfection of the divine realm.

choices.content: an expression of the perfection of the divine realm.
an expression of the torments of hell.
an expression of the underlying chaos of the universe

assessment_items.question_content:
**Leonardo's letter to the Duke of Milan tells us that court artists were engaged in many activities, including engineering, not just painting and sculpting.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Court artists did many jobs, which could include making canons and designing military defenses, not just painting and sculpting. Court artists might also be involved in agriculture and the transportation of water.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Pope Julius II financed his art and architecture projects in part through military campaigns.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Pope Julius II financed projects such as the rebuilding of St. Peters through his military campaigns.
